title: Vasquez Named to Rare Disease Coalition Advisory Board
body:
Dr. Lena Vasquez, who recently joined Halcyon Genomics after the acquisition of her company Oryza Therapeutics, has been appointed to the advisory board of the National Rare Disease Coalition, the group announced Tuesday.

Vasquez fills a seat vacated by a researcher who moved to industry last year. The coalition, a patient advocacy and policy organization, advises federal agencies on research funding priorities and reimbursement frameworks for orphan drugs.

In a brief statement, Vasquez said she hoped to bring "a founder's perspective and a scientist's rigor" to the organization's work. Her CRISPR platform at Oryza is one of a handful of gene-editing programs targeting inherited metabolic conditions.

Halcyon's CEO, Dominic Ferrara, described the appointment as evidence of Vasquez's broader influence in the rare disease community. The company has been working to integrate Oryza's team following the deal's close earlier this month.

The National Rare Disease Coalition has been active this year in lobbying for expanded FDA accelerated approval pathways for small-population diseases. Its annual meeting is scheduled for April in Washington, D.C., where Vasquez is expected to speak.
